title: Python memory prompt runtime delegate reduction

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Audit whether memory.prompt.render has a safe bounded generated-runtime reduction available without copying prompt policy, source-resolution, or local-tool selection logic into generated code.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not move prompt wording policy, recorded upgrade-source resolution, local runner selection, or legacy layout detection into generated code by copying source mechanics; if no small template primitive slice exists, close this as no-safe-route and continue #892 elsewhere.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Close this slice as no-safe-route: memory.prompt remains explicit package-domain runtime debt until prompt templates/source-resolution are represented by portable IR primitives.
completion_criteria: No generated-code reduction is implemented for memory.prompt.render.
The closeout records why prompt rendering is package-domain policy rather than a small deterministic fast path.
The #892 decomposition remains open for the next implementation-safe runtime delegate reduction.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open.
